request.question: Shownu, Kihyun, Hyungwon, Wonho, Minhyuk, and I.M. and what other South Korean comprise a group created in 2015?

title: Chocolat (band)
Lee Min-hyuk
Block B
Wonho Chung
Miss Me?
Lee Joo-heon
Dal Shabet
Shin'a'in
Chrysalis (EP)
Monsta X
sentences: Chocolat (Hangul: 쇼콜라, "Syokolla"), commonly stylized as ChoColat, was a South Korean girl group created by Paramount Music in 2011.
The group's name stems from the idea that each member is reminiscent of a different type of chocolate.
The name of the group was initially going to be "Chocolate", but there was already a group with that name, so Paramount Music decided to use the French word "chocolat" instead.
The group consists of mixed race members.
The group became inactive in 2013.
Melanie confirmed in an interview that the group members' contracts had concluded in February 2017 and the group had disbanded.
Lee Min-hyuk (; born November 29, 1990), commonly known as Minhyuk or Heota, is a South Korean singer, rapper, songwriter and actor.
He is a member of the South Korean boy group BtoB.
He received multiple supporting roles in television series such as "A New Leaf" and "Sweet, Savage Family".
Block B (Korean: ) is a South Korean boy group created by Cho PD in 2011.
The group consists of seven members: Taeil, B-Bomb, Jaehyo, U-Kwon, Park Kyung, Zico and P.O.
Wonho Chung (Arabic: ونهو شونغ‎ ‎ , Korean: 정원호 ) is a comedian and television personality, born in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, and raised in Amman, Jordan, to a South Korean father and Vietnamese mother.
Although raised in an East/Southeast Asian household, Wonho was given an Arab upbringing, acquiring proficiency in five languages, including native Arabic and fluent English.
Miss Me?
(stylized as miss me?)

is the second and last extended play (EP) by South Korean girl group I.O.I, a project group created through the 2016 Mnet survival show, "Produce 101", composed of eleven trainees from different entertainment companies that promoted until January 2017 under YMC Entertainment.
It contains five tracks, including the lead single, "Very Very Very" produced by Park Jin-young.
Lee Joo-heon (born Lee Ho-joon on October 6, 1994), better known by the mononym Jooheon, is a South Korean rapper and songwriter.
He is a member of South Korean boy group Monsta X, formed during the 2014 Mnet's survival show "No.Mercy".
Dal Shabet (Hangul: 달샤벳 , stylized as Dal★Shabet or Dalshabet) is a South Korean girl group created by E-Tribe under Happy Face Entertainment.
The group is currently composed of four members: Serri, Ah Young, Woohee and Subin.
The group debuted on January 3, 2011, with the release of "Supa Dupa Diva".
The Shin'a'in (translation: "People of the Plains") are a fictional ethnographic group created by fantasy author Mercedes Lackey.
They comprise roughly half of the descendants of an ancient race, the Kaled'a'in; the other half are the Tale'edras ("Brothers of the Hawk").
They are characterized by their chosen homeland, the Dhorisha Plains, an enormous grassland in the bottom of a great crater, and by their refusal to use magic except as part of the shamanic rituals.
They resemble the Bedouins in lifestyle; they are nomadic, living in tents, and they are predominantly herders and horse-breeders.

Chrysalis is the debut extended play by South Korean girl group I.O.I, a special group created through the 2016 Mnet survival show, "Produce 101", composed of eleven trainees from different entertainment companies that will promote for a year under YMC Entertainment.
The album was released digitally on May 4, 2016 and physically on May 9 with the single "Dream Girls" in two versions, a standard edition and a special edition.
Monsta X (Hangul: 몬스타엑스; often stylized as MONSTA X) is a South Korean boy group formed by Starship Entertainment through the survival show "No.Mercy" on Mnet in 2015.
The group is composed of seven members: Jooheon, Shownu, Kihyun, Hyungwon, Wonho, Minhyuk, and I.M.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Task: Compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a single concise, retrieval-oriented intermediate state to guide the next-hop retrieval. Do not produce the final answer or perform any new retrieval here.

Required content of `summary` (single field):
- A short header identifying the primary topic(s) found in the passages (1 line).
- A concise enumeration of the concrete facts supported verbatim by the provided passages: list entity mentions and directly supported factual spans (dates, years, titles, roles). Use very short quoted fragments (<= ~15 words) only when those words appear verbatim in the passages. Keep to facts present in the passages; do not add or infer new facts.
- Explicitly expose any ambiguity or missing information that blocks answering: if multiple candidate entities or page variants appear, enumerate those candidates and label the name as ambiguous; if a required target fact is missing or ambiguous, label it as the missing fact and identify the bridge entity that connects this hop to the next.
- Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives (1–4 items): concrete page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, explicit article sections to inspect (e.g., lede/infobox, "Career", "Publication", "History"), or tight search keywords. Order them by priority and be specific (include possible disambiguation terms or section names).

Formatting and style constraints:
- Keep the entire `summary` concise: a short header plus 1–4 brief sentences or 2–6 short bullet lines in total.
- Preserve the habit of extracting and citing concrete facts verbatim from the passages (names, dates, passage labels).
- Do not output a final answer, comparison, or any numeric fact that purports to resolve the question here. Even if the passages fully and unambiguously contain all required facts, present that evidence and explicitly state "no additional retrieval needed," but defer producing any final answer to a downstream component.
- If a name or term has multiple distinct variants in the passages, do not pick one without disambiguating evidence; enumerate candidates instead.
- Do not invent or add facts not present in the supplied passages. Avoid long verbatim quotations; prefer short extracts (<= ~15 words) or paraphrase.

Output expectations: produce exactly one field `summary` containing the concise, retrieval-oriented summary that meets the above requirements.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions (must follow exactly):
- Emit a single field named `summary` containing only 1–3 concise sentences that integrate the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into a grounded post-retrieval state.
- If the `context` already contains a concise answer sentence, keep that sentence as the first sentence of `summary` unless the `passages` clearly contradict it; append supporting sentence(s) after it rather than replacing it.
- Preserve the primary answer name(s) exactly as they appear in the inputs (context or passages) unless those names clearly conflict with stronger passage content.
- When one or more `passages` supply short biographical, membership, partnership, or corroborating facts about a named entity from the `context`, append at most one brief supporting sentence per relevant named person. Each such supporting sentence should:
  - begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the passages (if available),
  - paraphrase up to one or two key attributes present in the passages (examples: nationality, award/winner status, current affiliation/team, partner identity, founding/formation fact, birth date), and
  - avoid long verbatim copying (prefer paraphrase; use short quotes only when a brief exact phrase from a passage is necessary for clarity).
- If the question requires naming both members of a partnership or collaborator pair, name both people in the summary and, if passages provide partner-specific attributes, include one brief attribute for each as separate short clauses or sentences if needed.
- Do not invent facts not present in the provided passages. If passages conflict on a specific attribute, do not synthesize a new reconciled fact; instead prefer the passage that most directly addresses the question or omit the disputed attribute (or state necessary ambiguity only when needed to avoid a false claim).
- Keep the `summary` compact and focused: avoid extraneous biography, discography/chart data, or unrelated commentary. When `context` is absent or is not a concise answer, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary naming the relevant entity(ies) and including at most one brief paraphrased attribute drawn from the passages.

Output requirement: exactly one JSON field `summary` whose value is the integrated 1–3 sentence summary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only `summary_1` and `summary_2` as the evidential basis for the final decision; do not add new facts or external information.
- First determine whether the question is a binary (yes/no) proposition and whether both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary outcome.
- If BOTH summaries unambiguously assert the same binary conclusion, emit exactly one lowercase token as the entire content of `answer`: use "yes" for an affirmative conclusion and "no" for a negative conclusion. The `answer` field must contain that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no extra whitespace, no explanation).
- Prefer `summary_2` as the decisive statement when it clarifies or restates `summary_1`, but still require that both summaries support the same binary judgment before emitting the single-token `answer`.
- If the summaries conflict, if either summary hedges, is ambiguous, silent about the binary relation, or otherwise fails to clearly agree, do NOT apply the single-token rule; instead produce the module's normal fallback/unresolved output for `answer`.
- Always remain truthful to the visible summaries and do not alter their factual conclusions.

response.hop1_query: Shownu, Kihyun, Hyungwon, Wonho, Minhyuk, and I.M. and what other South Korean comprise a group created in 2015?
hop1_docs: Monsta X | Monsta X (Hangul: 몬스타엑스; often stylized as MONSTA X) is a South Korean boy group formed by Starship Entertainment through the survival show "No.Mercy" on Mnet in 2015.  The group is composed of seven members: Jooheon, Shownu, Kihyun, Hyungwon, Wonho, Minhyuk, and I.M.

Wonho Chung | Wonho Chung (Arabic: ونهو شونغ‎ ‎ , Korean: 정원호 ) is a comedian and television personality, born in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, and raised in Amman, Jordan, to a South Korean father and Vietnamese mother.  Although raised in an East/Southeast Asian household, Wonho was given an Arab upbringing, acquiring proficiency in five languages, including native Arabic and fluent English.

Lee Min-hyuk | Lee Min-hyuk (; born November 29, 1990), commonly known as Minhyuk or Heota, is a South Korean singer, rapper, songwriter and actor.  He is a member of the South Korean boy group BtoB.  He received multiple supporting roles in television series such as "A New Leaf" and "Sweet, Savage Family".

What's Twice? | What's Twice?  is the first compilation extended play by South Korean girl group Twice.  It was released exclusively in Japan on February 24, 2017 by Warner Music Japan and features four of their Korean songs from their first three extended plays: "The Story Begins" (2015), "Page Two" (2016) and "" (2016).
Ki Hyun Ryu | Ki Hyun Ryu or Kihyun Ryu is a Korean-American television director of animated cartoons.

Jewelry (band) | Jewelry (Korean: 쥬얼리 ) was a South Korean girl group formed in 2001 by Star Empire Entertainment.  The group was the longest-running Korean girl group (with more than 2 members) until their disbandment in 2015, surpassing super-group Baby V.O.X.

GS Group | GS Group is a South Korean conglomerate.  GS comprises GS Holdings, subsidiaries & affiliates including GS Caltex, GS Retail, GS Shop, GS EPS, GS Global, GS Sports and GS E&C among others.  The asset size is at 31.1 trillion KRW as the end of 2007 placing GS at the 6th largest in Korea excluding the public business companies.
response.summary_1:
Monsta X — formation and membership (2015)

- Passage [1]: "Monsta X ... is a South Korean boy group formed ... in 2015" and formed by "Starship Entertainment" via survival show "'No.Mercy' on Mnet".
- Passage [1] lists the group's members: "composed of seven members: Jooheon, Shownu, Kihyun, Hyungwon, Wonho, Minhyuk, and I.M."
- Ambiguities: "Wonho" appears also as "Wonho Chung" in passage [2] (different person); "Minhyuk" appears as "Lee Min-hyuk" of BtoB in passage [3] (different person). Label these as name-variant ambiguities.
- Next-hop retrieval (priority): 1) No additional retrieval needed (passage [1] contains the full member list). 2) If verification of individual biographies is desired, fetch the "Monsta X" page lede/infobox and the individual member pages/sections (e.g., "Members" or each member's article). 3) Optionally fetch disambiguation or biography pages for "Wonho" and "Minhyuk" to resolve name-variant ambiguity.
hop2_docs: Monsta X | Monsta X (Hangul: 몬스타엑스; often stylized as MONSTA X) is a South Korean boy group formed by Starship Entertainment through the survival show "No.Mercy" on Mnet in 2015.  The group is composed of seven members: Jooheon, Shownu, Kihyun, Hyungwon, Wonho, Minhyuk, and I.M.

Monsta Island Czars discography | This is the solo and group discography of the legendary underground hip hop collective Monsta Island Czars.  Included are the solo studio albums, EPs and compilations of the various group members and affiliates.  Although Monsta Island Czars aren't formed until after the death of Subroc, KMD's albums Mr. Hood and Black Bastards are stylistic precursors to what will appear in the Monsta Island Czars catalog.
Monsta Island Czars | Monsta Island Czars is a hip-hop collective based in New York City, New York, United States most notable for members MF Grimm and MF Doom.

Escape from Monsta Island! | Escape from Monsta Island!  is a studio album released by the Monsta Island Czars, a collective notably featuring MF Doom under his King Geedorah alias.  Although the Monsta Island Czars was founded by MF Grimm, he is absent on the entire album due to his incarceration at the time of its production and release.

Monsta X discography | The South Korean hip-hop boy group Monsta X, produced and managed by Starship Entertainment debuted in 2015 and have currently released one studio album, four extended plays, one compilation album, one video album, over ten music videos and eleven singles.  The group and members have also participated in singing nine soundtracks for various Korean dramas.

Monsta (EP) | Monsta is the debut extended play (EP) by the British band I See MONSTAS (credited as MONSTA on the release).  It was released on 23 October 2012 through OWSLA.  The EP spawned a single, "Messiah", which entered the UK Singles Chart at number 154.  "Holdin' On" climbed the UK Singles Chart to number 48, due to strong sales of the Skrillex and Nero remix.  It also peaked at number ten on UK Dance Chart and number three the UK Indie Chart.

I See Monstas | I See Monstas (often stylised I See MONSTAS, and previously called MONSTA), are a British electronic music group.  The group was formed in 2012 and consists of producers Rocky and Rufio (also known collectively as Pegasus) as well as singer Bryn Christopher (also known as Skaar).  The trio have officially remixed Labrinth ("Last Time"), Zedd ("Spectrum"), and Tinie Tempah ("Trampoline") among others.  They have also produced for Ellie Goulding the songs "Figure 8" and "Ritual" from her second studio album "Halcyon".
response.summary_2: The group is composed of seven members: Jooheon, Shownu, Kihyun, Hyungwon, Wonho, Minhyuk, and I.M. Monsta X was formed by Starship Entertainment in 2015 through the Mnet survival show "No.Mercy".
